p.seg: William king of the Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, French and English and Scots, greeting.

Let those to come as well as those present know me to have licensed and by this my charter to have made firm to the church of Saint Mary at Melrose and the monks in that place serving God the donation that Walter son of Alan made, to the same church and to the monks, in respect of the land of 'Edmundestoun'; in all things so freely and quitely in everlasting alms, as freely and quitely the charter of Walter himself to them bears witness.

Witness Richard, Bishop of St Andrews; Ingram, bishop of Glasgow; John, abbot of Kelso; Osbert, abbot of Jedburgh; Nicholas the chancellor; Richard the chaplain; Richard de Morville; Walter son of Alan; Philip de Valognes; Robert Avenel; Walter Corbet; Gilbert son of Richer; William de la Haye; William de Mortimer; Ralph le Nain; Imer the clerk; Walter of Berkeley. At Lanark.
